One Link
How it works Features Security Live mesh Share About Get One Link
Share a file

Drop a file. Send the link. Only they can read it.

Drop any file up to 25 MB. We encrypt it in your browser, store the ciphertext for 24 hours, and give you a link. The key lives in the link itself. We never see it. The first person to open the link gets the file, then it is gone.

Drop a file here or tap to choose. Up to 25 MB.
What just happened, under the hood
Incoming file

Someone sent you something.

The file was encrypted in their browser. The key is in your URL, never on our server. Click to decrypt and download. The file deletes itself after this.

Encrypted before it leaves.

Your browser generates a fresh 256-bit AES-GCM key. The file is sealed in your tab. The ciphertext goes to our server. The key never does.

The key is in the link.

The URL we give you has the key in the fragment (the bit after #). Browsers never send fragments to servers. Only the person you send the link to can derive it.

Self-destructs.

The first person to open the link gets the file, then it deletes from our server. If nobody opens it, it deletes in 24 hours anyway. Nothing persists.

This is what One Link does, made obvious in a browser. Get One Link for the same on every device, without the size limit, without us in the middle at all.

Get One Link →
One Link

A free, private network for messages, files, and devices. Owned by no one. Belongs to everyone.

Built in the open. AGPL-3.0. No tracking, no analytics, no cookies. we are one
